seoTitle: How to Import Issues From Excel to Jira
metaDescription: Import a spreadsheet of issues from Excel into Jira, mapping columns to fields so bulk data loads in correctly.
intro: Migrating from a spreadsheet to Jira, or bulk-adding a large batch of issues, is much faster through the built-in import tool than creating each one by hand. This guide covers importing issues from Excel into Jira.

steps.heading: Prepare your Excel file
steps.body: Format your spreadsheet with clear column headers matching, as closely as possible, the Jira fields you want to populate.

steps.heading: Save the file as CSV
steps.body: Convert the Excel file to CSV format, since Jira's external import tool typically expects CSV input.

steps.heading: Open the external system import
steps.body: Go to Jira administration and find the CSV import option under system settings.

steps.heading: Map your columns to Jira fields
steps.body: Match each spreadsheet column to the corresponding Jira field, such as summary, description, or assignee.

steps.heading: Run the import and verify results
steps.body: Start the import, then check the created issues to confirm data landed in the right fields.

faq.question: Does Jira support importing directly from an XLSX file?
faq.answer: Jira's import tool generally requires CSV format, so an Excel file typically needs to be saved as CSV first.

faq.question: Can I import issues into an existing project?
faq.answer: Yes, the import wizard lets you choose an existing project as the destination for the imported issues.

faq.question: What happens if a column doesn't match any Jira field?
faq.answer: Unmatched columns can usually be mapped to a custom field or simply left unmapped and ignored during import.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many issues I can import at once?
faq.answer: Very large imports may need to be split into smaller batches to avoid timeouts or performance issues.
